articles.title: Connelly Early named to Red Sox starting rotation
articles.description:
The move will reportedly be reevaluated "after a couple turns through the rotation."

The post Connelly Early named to Red Sox starting rotation appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Connelly Early officially joins the Red Sox starting rotation as of March 24, 2026.
The decision will be reevaluated after a few games in the rotation.
Boston fans can expect Early's performance to influence team dynamics this season.

Boston residents may find affordable travel options to Portland for Easter festivities.

articles.title: Netflix star Emma Hernan is opening a frozen food facility in Hingham
articles.description:
The “Selling Sunset” star’s new South Shore facility will produce items for her frozen food brand, Coast to Coast Kitchen.

summary: Emma Hernan opens a frozen food facility in Hingham for her brand Coast to Coast Kitchen.
The facility aims to produce a variety of frozen food items for local distribution.
This development is expected to create new jobs and boost the local economy in Hingham.

articles.title: How neighborly do home additions have to be?

As municipalities encourage more housing density, neighbors debate what kind of construction should be allowed.

summary: Boston municipalities push for increased housing density amid neighbor disputes over home additions.

articles.title: This mental health worker is running Boston for her neighbors in South Boston
articles.description:
"When we run together, we rise together."

The post This mental health worker is running Boston for her neighbors in South Boston appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Mental health worker Sarha Clark is running the Boston Marathon to support South Boston residents.
Clark aims to raise awareness for mental health issues in her community during the April 2026 marathon.
Her initiative promotes community solidarity and encourages local residents to prioritize mental health.

articles.title: Another crop of Boston College NFL hopefuls get a chance to shine at Eagles’ annual Pro Day
articles.description:
With Boston College nearby, and Bill O’Brien as a close friend, Mike Vrabel and the Patriots have plenty of intel when it comes to monitoring local players potentially worth drafting. Even so, it never hurts to compile additional data, and Monday’s Pro Day gave the Patriots — along with 29 other teams — another opportunity […]

The post Another crop of Boston College NFL hopefuls get a chance to shine at Eagles’ annual Pro Day appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Boston College hosted its annual Pro Day, attracting 30 NFL teams including the Patriots.
Mike Vrabel and Bill O’Brien provided insights on local talent during the event.
Local players showcased their skills, increasing their chances of being drafted in the NFL.

articles.title: Lowell was named the best place to live in Mass. But why?
articles.description:

summary: Lowell was named the best place to live in Massachusetts for its vibrant culture.

Local leaders emphasize opportunities for growth and development in Lowell.

articles.title: Body of missing Holy Cross student recovered in Connecticut lake
articles.description:
Authorities say the 20-year-old was found after a multi-day search following a canoe accident on Lake Hayward in East Haddam.

The post Body of missing Holy Cross student recovered in Connecticut lake appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Authorities recovered the body of a missing Holy Cross student, age 20, from Lake Hayward.
The student went missing after a canoe accident on March 24, prompting a multi-day search.
This tragedy impacts the Holy Cross community and raises safety concerns for local outdoor activities.

articles.title: Livestream: Testimony resumes in Kelsey Fitzsimmons’s trial
articles.description:
Fitzsimmons, a North Andover police officer, was shot by a colleague in her home last summer. She's pleaded not guilty to a charge of assault with a dangerous weapon.

The post Livestream: Testimony resumes in Kelsey Fitzsimmons’s trial appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Testimony resumes in Kelsey Fitzsimmons's trial for assault charges on March 24, 2026.
Fitzsimmons, a North Andover officer, was shot by a colleague in her home last summer.
The trial's outcome may influence local police conduct and community trust in law enforcement.
